perturbation.original_text: EXCEPTIONS FROM CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Term “Confidential information” does NOT include: - Matter of public or otherwise freely accessible knowledge, resulting from direct disclosure by the Owne r - Information rightfully received by the Recipient from a Third party without any duty of confidentiality - Independently developed information on behalf of the Recipient - Already d isclosed information for the purposes of court or civil proceedings, respecting the principle of “minimal disclosure” - Information disclosed on behalf of the Recipient with prior written or oral ascent from the Owner
perturbation.changed_text: EXCEPTIONS FROM CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Term “Confidential information” does NOT include: - Matter of public or otherwise freely accessible knowledge, resulting from direct disclosure by the Owne r, unless such disclosure was accidental or inadvertent - Information rightfully received by the Recipient from a Third party without any duty of confidentiality - Independently developed information on behalf of the Recipient - Already d isclosed information for the purposes of court or civil proceedings, respecting the principle of “minimal disclosure” - Information disclosed on behalf of the Recipient with prior written or oral ascent from the Owner
perturbation.explanation: The modification adds an exception to the exception, stating that if public knowledge results from accidental disclosure by the Owner, it is *still* considered confidential. This introduces a contradiction because the original clause explicitly excludes matters of public knowledge. Now, the definition of 'Confidential Information' is unclear, as accidentally disclosed information is simultaneously considered public knowledge and confidential. This creates an internal contradiction if, for example, the 'Damages Due to Disclosure' section is invoked.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXCEPTIONS FROM CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION The Term “Confidential information” does NOT include: - Matter of public or otherwise freely accessible knowledge, resulting from direct disclosure by the Owne r, unless such disclosure was accidental or inadvertent - Information rightfully received by the Recipient from a Third party without any duty of confidentiality - Independently developed information on behalf of the Recipient - Already d isclosed information for the purposes of court or civil proceedings, respecting the principle of “minimal disclosure” - Information disclosed on behalf of the Recipient with prior written or oral ascent from the Owner
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original exception within the 'EXCEPTIONS FROM CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION' section. Specifically, it states that public knowledge resulting from accidental disclosure by the Owner is *still* considered confidential. This directly contradicts the initial statement that 'Matter of public or otherwise freely accessible knowledge, resulting from direct disclosure by the Owner' is NOT considered confidential information. The added clause makes the definition of confidential information ambiguous, satisfying the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION On demand, at any time and at any event which may occur the Recipient is obl iged to immediately deliver to the Owner all the date, manuals, lists, notes, writings, product lists, photocopies, recordings, disks or other material (including duplicates and/or copies of any such property and/or material) concerning the work at hand. T he Recipient understands and respects that the Owner has all proprietary rights to these materials, and that these materials are entrusted to the Recipient on basis of execution of contract tied to this Non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION On demand, only if requested in writing, at any time and at any event which may occur the Recipient is obl iged to immediately deliver to the Owner all the date, manuals, lists, notes, writings, product lists, photocopies, recordings, disks or other material (including duplicates and/or copies of any such property and/or material) concerning the work at hand. T he Recipient understands and respects that the Owner has all proprietary rights to these materials, and that these materials are entrusted to the Recipient on basis of execution of contract tied to this Non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the clause 'only if requested in writing,' the provision regarding returning confidential information now conflicts with the original unconditional demand. Initially, the Recipient was obliged to return materials 'on demand, at any time.' Now, the demand must be in writing. This affects the 'RIGHT TO INJUCTIVE RELIEF' section, as an oral demand may not trigger the right to seek an injunction, creating an in-text contradiction about the conditions required for information return.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION On demand, only if requested in writing, at any time and at any event which may occur the Recipient is obl iged to immediately deliver to the Owner all the date, manuals, lists, notes, writings, product lists, photocopies, recordings, disks or other material (including duplicates and/or copies of any such property and/or material) concerning the work at hand. T he Recipient understands and respects that the Owner has all proprietary rights to these materials, and that these materials are entrusted to the Recipient on basis of execution of contract tied to this Non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the clause 'only if requested in writing', modifying the condition for returning confidential information. Originally, the Recipient was obliged to return materials 'on demand, at any time.' The modified text now stipulates that the demand must be in writing. This change introduces a direct contradiction because the original text implies an unconditional obligation while the changed text makes the obligation conditional on a written request. This creates a clear and direct conflict, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability, which fits the definition of an in-text contradiction.
